nodes.label: Design System Generation Workflow
nodes.rationale: Always start UI/UX work with --design-system to search five domains in parallel and apply ui-reasoning.csv rules for pattern, style, colors, typography, effects, and anti-patterns before implementation.

nodes.label: Master + Overrides Design System Pattern
nodes.rationale: Persist design systems as design-system/MASTER.md with page-specific overrides in design-system/pages/; page files override Master when present for hierarchical retrieval across sessions.

nodes.label: Engine_block
nodes.source_location: right of cylinder with gauge icon

nodes.label: Haptic Telegraph 3D CAD Model
nodes.rationale: Isometric 3D CAD rendering of the Haptic Telegraph haptic teleoperation rig used as portfolio project hero imagery.

nodes.label: Haptic Telegraph Device
nodes.rationale: Mechanical-electronic assembly for force-feedback teleoperation with dual articulated manipulator arms and embedded control electronics.

nodes.label: Transparent Protective Cover
nodes.rationale: Clear translucent upper casing exposes internal electronics while protecting components.

nodes.label: Internal Mounting Plate
nodes.rationale: Flat gray plate inside the enclosure engraved with HAPTIC TELEGRAPH; serves as mounting surface for modules and user input.

nodes.label: Pivot Hinge Joint
nodes.rationale: Circular hinge mechanism with metallic screws allowing each arm to rotate relative to the main body.

nodes.label: 5 kg Load Cell
nodes.rationale: Black square sensor at each arm tip labeled 5 kg with downward arrow indicating force measurement up to 5 kg.

nodes.label: Finger Interaction Pad
nodes.rationale: White oval pad on top of each arm for operator finger placement during haptic interaction.

nodes.label: Teensy Microcontroller PCB
nodes.rationale: Green main circuit board mounted toward the rear with chips and a micro-USB port; central embedded controller for the rig.

nodes.label: Green Breakout Board
nodes.rationale: Two smaller green PCBs visible inside the enclosure, likely sensor or communication interface boards.

nodes.label: Green Push Button Input
nodes.rationale: Prominent green cylindrical push button mounted vertically on the internal plate for user input.

nodes.label: Black Square Module
nodes.rationale: Square black component mounted near center of internal plate; likely a sensor or power module.

nodes.label: Signal Wiring Harness
nodes.rationale: Colored wires (red, green, black, white) visible at arm pivot points connecting arm sensors to internal electronics.

nodes.label: Slotted Head Screws
nodes.rationale: Multiple slotted screws secure pivot joints, internal plate, and mounted modules throughout the assembly.

nodes.label: Blue Cylindrical Shaft Coupler or Sensor
nodes.norm_label: blue cylindrical shaft coupler or sensor

nodes.label: Vertical Grey Support Bracket with Circular Cutout
nodes.norm_label: vertical grey support bracket with circular cutout

nodes.label: Side-Mounted Carburetor with Yellow Fuel Inlet
nodes.norm_label: side-mounted carburetor with yellow fuel inlet

nodes.label: Tuned Exhaust Pipe with Yellow Pressure Fitting
nodes.norm_label: tuned exhaust pipe with yellow pressure fitting

nodes.label: Silver L-Shaped Support Bracket with Circular Face
nodes.norm_label: silver l-shaped support bracket with circular face

nodes.label: Orange Base Block with Rectangular Cutouts
nodes.norm_label: orange base block with rectangular cutouts

nodes.label: Portfolio Favicon Logo
nodes.rationale: Minimal SVG favicon sized for browser tabs and bookmarks; encodes personal brand identity in a 32x32 canvas.

nodes.label: Bold Letter B Monogram
nodes.rationale: Centered single-letter monogram using system-ui bold typography as the primary recognizable mark.

nodes.label: Cream Accent Dot
nodes.rationale: Small circular accent at lower-right adds visual balance and echoes the cream accent color used on the letterform.

nodes.label: mmWave Radar Hardware Photo
nodes.rationale: Portfolio photograph of a wall-mounted mmWave radar fall-detection prototype showing production-style hardware installation.

nodes.label: White mmWave Radar Sensor Device
nodes.rationale: Matte-white rectangular enclosure with rounded corners, mounted horizontally on a wall.

nodes.label: Radar Sensor Aperture Window
nodes.rationale: Small dark square inset on the right face of the enclosure; optical aperture for the mmWave transceiver module.

nodes.label: Vertical Cable Concealer Conduit
nodes.rationale: White semi-cylindrical wall channel routing the cable downward for a tidy install.

nodes.label: Clean Cable Routing Design
nodes.rationale: Cable exits bottom-left of the device and is concealed in a vertical conduit, indicating deployment-oriented industrial design rather than bench prototype wiring.

nodes.label: Contactless mmWave Presence Sensing
nodes.rationale: Wall-mounted radar module enables non-camera, privacy-preserving motion and presence detection in occupied spaces.

nodes.label: mmWave Radar Hardware Photo — Alternate View
nodes.rationale: Indoor photograph documenting a wall-mounted mmWave radar sensor installation with concealed cable routing — alternate deployment view of the fall-detection prototype.
nodes.norm_label: mmwave radar hardware photo — alternate view

nodes.label: mmWave Radar Sensor Unit
nodes.rationale: Small white rectangular device with rounded corners mounted horizontally; front face exposes a dark recessed sensor window for millimeter-wave ranging.

nodes.label: Radar Sensor Aperture
nodes.rationale: Dark rectangular recessed window on the sensor front face; the active mmWave transmit/receive aperture for point-cloud capture.

nodes.label: White Power Cable
nodes.rationale: Thin white cable exits the bottom center of the radar unit via a visible connector and routes downward toward concealed trunking.

nodes.label: Vertical Cable Conduit Raceway
nodes.rationale: Flush-mounted white plastic raceway running vertically down the wall to neatly conceal the power cable run.

nodes.label: Wall-Mount Field Deployment
nodes.rationale: Shows the sensor installed in a real indoor environment at occupant height with professional cable management, representing deployable hardware rather than bench prototyping.

nodes.label: mmWave Fall-Detection Device
nodes.rationale: Portfolio project context: privacy-preserving fall detection using mmWave radar and point-cloud processing on Raspberry Pi at Ambient Intelligence.
